ops.text: codex-wrapper agent (spawned from a Workflow via agentType) launched its pinned codex exec as a run_in_background Bash task and then ended its turn with "Waiting for the background codex task to finish" — the workflow's pipeline treated that string as the implementation result, ran the gate on an untouched tree (trivially green), and the refuter correctly found no diff. The codex process itself survived as a detached orphan (still running, danger-full-access, editing the workspace after the workflow reported complete). Same family as the 2026-07-13 preamble-fragment papercut: a wrapper's final text is trusted as the result with no completed-work check. Mitigation that works: instruct the wrapper prompt explicitly to run codex exec in the FOREGROUND and never via run_in_background; orchestrator should also verify the working copy actually changed before trusting an implementation lane's "done".
